→ If it's powerful, useful,
or underrated — you'll see it here.

📩 DM for collaborations

Created: 2026-06-28T15:05:46.000Z
Metrics: {"retweet_count":19,"reply_count":10,"like_count":67,"quote_count":3,"bookmark_count":56,"impression_count":17526}
Article content: {"title":"The Ultimate AI Second Brain (2026): Connect Claude to Obsidian Once—Never Repeat Yourself Again","cover_media":"3_2071248550051373056","plain_text":"Most people don't have an AI problem.

They have a context problem.

Every new chat starts the same way:

- Re-explaining your project.
- Re-uploading files.
- Repeating goals.
- Searching through old conversations.
- Forgetting ideas you had last month.

Hours disappear rebuilding context that already existed.

The solution isn't a better prompt.

It's giving Claude a permanent memory.

Not fake memory.

A real knowledge base you own.

Here's how to build it.

---

Step 1 — Understand the Architecture

Think of it like this:

Obsidian = Your Brain

- Stores every note as Markdown files.
- Everything stays on your computer.
- No vendor lock-in.
- Notes connect through backlinks.

Claude = Your Research Partner

- Reads your entire vault.
- Writes new notes.
- Updates existing knowledge.
- Connects ideas automatically.
- Answers questions across years of information.

Instead of chatting with an empty AI every day...

You're chatting with one that already knows your work.

---

Step 2 — Install Claude Code

Regular Claude chats can't manage your local files.

Claude Code can.

It reads folders, edits documents, organizes projects, generates reports, and works directly inside your computer.

This becomes the engine behind your second brain.

---

Step 3 — Create Your Obsidian Vault

Create one vault.

Name it anything:

- Brain
- Second Brain
- Knowledge
- OS
- Vault

Everything goes here.

Ideas.

Meeting notes.

Books.

Research.

Code snippets.

Personal knowledge.

Journal.

Projects.

Unlike cloud note apps...

Every file is simply Markdown.

Meaning you'll still own your knowledge ten years from now.

---

Step 4 — Connect Claude to the Vault

Using MCP (Model Context Protocol), Claude gains secure access to your vault.

Instead of copying notes into chat...

Claude simply reads them directly.

Want a summary?

It already has it.

Need yesterday's meeting?

It already knows where it lives.

Searching becomes unnecessary.

---

Step 5 — Build Your Personal Knowledge Profile

Don't spend hours writing documentation.

Interview yourself.

Ask Claude to learn:

- Who you are
- What you're building
- Career goals
- Communication preferences
- Strengths
- Weaknesses
- Current projects
- Long-term plans

Then save everything into one master file.

From that point onward...

Every conversation starts with context.

---

Step 6 — Organize Like an AI Researcher

Use the same workflow many advanced AI users follow.

raw/

Original information.

- PDFs
- Books
- Articles
- YouTube transcripts
- Screenshots
- Research papers
- Documentation

Never edit these.

They're your source of truth.

wiki/

Claude creates this automatically.

For every new source it can:

- Write summaries
- Build concept pages
- Link related ideas
- Remove duplicate information
- Keep everything connected

Over time...

Your vault becomes a living encyclopedia.

---

Step 7 — Teach Claude Obsidian's Native Language

Out of the box, Claude only understands Markdown.

Teach it Obsidian features like:

- Wikilinks
- Callouts
- Canvas
- Bases
- Templates
- Graph relationships

Suddenly every note feels hand-written instead of AI-generated.

---

Step 8 — Connect Real-Time Data

Static notes are useful.

Live information is powerful.

Connect services like:

- Google Calendar
- Gmail
- GitHub
- Slack
- Linear
- Notion
- Local folders

Now Claude can automatically:

- Summarize meetings
- Extract action items
- Update project notes
- Track deadlines
- Generate daily reports

No manual organization required.

---

Step 9 — Automate Everything

The real magic happens when you're asleep.

Schedule Claude to run every morning.

Imagine waking up to:

- New research already summarized
- Fresh notes linked together
- Dead links repaired
- Tasks updated
- Project progress tracked
- Daily briefing waiting for you

You didn't organize anything.

The system did.

---

Step 10 — Start with Community Templates

Don't build everything yourself.

The open-source community has already created complete second-brain systems.

They include:

- Ready-made vault structures
- Automation workflows
- AI skills
- Commands
- Templates
- Knowledge organization systems

Clone one.

Customize it.

Make it yours.

---

What Changes After a Few Months?

Before:

• You search.

• Claude forgets.

• Knowledge gets buried.

• Ideas disappear.

• Every project starts from zero.

After:

• Claude remembers your work.

• Every note connects itself.

• Research compounds instead of disappearing.

• Projects never lose context.

• Your knowledge becomes searchable in seconds.

• Every new conversation starts exactly where the last one ended.

The longer you use it...

The smarter your system becomes.

Not because Claude is getting smarter.

Because your knowledge keeps compounding.

That's the real advantage.

Most people use AI as a chatbot.

The top 1% use it as an operating system for their knowledge.

Once you experience that...

You'll never want to start a blank chat again.","preview_text":"Most people don't have an AI problem.

They have a context problem.

Every new chat starts the same way:

- Re-explaining your project.
- Re-uploading files.
- Repeating goals.
- Searching through old"}
